informal: If $z_1, \ldots, z_n$ are complex, prove that $|z_1 + z_2 + \ldots + z_n| \leq |z_1| + |z_2| + \cdots + |z_n|$.
lean:
theorem Rudin_exercise_1_12 (n : ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℂ) :
  ‖∑ i ∈ range n, f i‖ ≤ ∑ i ∈ range n, ‖f i‖
reasoning: The informal statement is the triangle inequality for a finite sum of complex numbers. The Lean version states ‖∑ i ∈ range n, f i‖ ≤ ∑ i ∈ range n, ‖f i‖ for any function f : ℕ → ℂ and any n : ℕ. This is logically equivalent: indexing n complex numbers z_1,...,z_n by a function f : ℕ → ℂ over Finset.range n captures exactly the same finite sum. The norm ‖·‖ on ℂ equals the absolute value. So the Lean statement faithfully encodes the claim.
